name: Blackeyed Peas and Rice
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook 1 cup Lipton Rice 'n' Sauce Cajun.
2. Add 1 tablespoon pepper and 1 cup stew meat.
3. Add 1 teaspoon Creole or Cajun seasoning.
4. Add 1 bell pepper, cayenne pepper, or hot sauce.
5. Rinse peas thoroughly and wash through three waters.
6. Soak peas in water overnight in the fridge.
7. The next day, discard the soaking water; some claim this keeps beans from causing issues, but stay on this diet for a couple of weeks to avoid problems.
8. Wash stew meat and put it with pre-soaked peas in a big pot on the stove, bringing to boil with plenty of water.
9. Add seasonings to taste. If using Cajun seasoning (which contains salt), do not add extra salt; otherwise, add salt to taste.
10. Reduce heat to medium once boiling and keep adding water as needed to avoid scorching.
11. After about 40 minutes, add the packet of Rice and Sauce (preferably Cajun flavor).
12. Keep a close eye on the water level and add more as necessary.
13. After about 20 minutes of rice cooking, add chopped bell pepper and onion, along with additional seasonings if needed.
14. Everything should be ready at the same time; take a test bite to ensure everything is cooked properly. Enjoy!
